headline: Broadway Etiquette Erupts: Actor Calls Out Disruptive Audience Behavior
lede: Stage veteran Isa Briones confronts the growing problem of audience disruption during live performances, highlighting rising tensions between performers and theatergoers.
body:
Broadway's unspoken social contract took center stage this weekend when Isa Briones, star of both TV's "The Pitt" and the current jukebox musical "Just In Time," publicly challenged audience members crossing the line of acceptable theater conduct.

Briones, currently portraying legendary singer Connie Francis in the Broadway production, used her social media platform to directly address fans who apparently interrupted her performance with excessive vocal outbursts. Her sharply worded critique underscores a mounting frustration among performers about maintaining professional performance environments.

The incident reveals a broader tension in live entertainment spaces, where fan enthusiasm increasingly conflicts with performers' need for artistic concentration. Theater professionals have long battled audience disruptions, from ill-timed phone usage to vocal interruptions that break the delicate fourth wall.

While Briones did not specify exact details of the disruption, her public callout suggests a significant breach of theater etiquette. Her dual career in television and theater provides her a unique platform to address these emerging performance challenges.

Industry observers note that social media has transformed how performers can directly communicate performance expectations, giving artists unprecedented ability to shape audience behavior in real-time.
key_facts: Isa Briones stars in Broadway's "Just In Time"
Actor publicly criticized disruptive audience behavior
Incident highlights growing tension in live performance spaces
Performer used social media to address theater etiquette
